top_signals.title: Oil Ticks Higher as Traders Await Signing of US-Iran Deal
top_signals.summary: Oil ticked up after two days of stark declines, with prices still broadly pressured by a pending interim peace deal between the US and Iran that could reopen the Strait of Hormuz and send millions of barrels back to the market.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.
